text:
Baselings is a pet game where you buy an egg, feed it groceries, and it poops.

That's it. That's the game.

The poop fuels everything.
notes: Absurd hook. Ethics-adjusted: 'fuels everything' replaces 'worth money'.

text:
My Baseling ate 4 burgers and a salad today. It grew 3 strength levels and then pooped.

I have never been more emotionally attached to a pixel creature.
notes: Personal diary humor. Ethics: PASS as-is.

text:
Pet game tier list:

S -- Baselings (pets poop game fuel)
A -- Tamagotchi (nostalgia carry)
B -- Neopets (RIP)
C -- that fish screensaver from 2004

Your pet literally produces fuel for the whole economy. Feed it, keep it alive, collect the output.
notes: Tier list humor. Ethics-adjusted: 'game fuel' replaces 'real tokens'.

text:
Things you can do in Baselings:

- Crack open a mystery egg
- Go grocery shopping for your pet
- Watch it level up from eating burgers
- Decorate its house
- Neglect it and watch it die in 3 days

It's Tamagotchi with consequences.

If you don't feed your Baseling for 3 days, it dies.

No undo. No revive. Gone.

The food is cheap. The guilt is not.

Baselings is a pet game on Base. Here's how it works.

You buy an egg. It could be common, rare, or if you're lucky, legendary. You don't know until it hatches. More attention you give it before hatching, better the odds.

Once it hatches, you feed it. Different foods grow different stats.

Burgers build strength. Salads build wisdom. Rice is balanced. There are 8 food types total, each with different stat profiles.

Your pet's diet determines what it becomes.

Fed pets produce POOP. Yes, really.

POOP is only created by Baselings eating. Nobody mints it. No team allocation. No pre-mine. The only source is a pet digesting food. If all pets die, production stops.

Keep your pet alive. Fuel the garden.
notes: Ethics-adjusted: 'Fuel the garden' replaces 'Collect the output'.

text:
The long game: your pet evolves based on what you fed it.

Different diets unlock different evolution paths. A burger-fed tank evolves differently than a salad-fed sage. Multiple pets means multiple builds.

Buy egg. Feed pet. Watch it grow. That's Baselings.

What can you actually do in Baselings right now? A lot more than you'd expect from a pet game.

Here's everything from egg to endgame.

Start: pick a mystery egg or a sorted egg (locked to a color/type). Tap it, care for it, wait for the hatch. Rarity rolls from egg tier plus your care level. A common egg with max care can still surprise you.

Middle: grocery store has 8 foods. Each grows different stats. You're building a creature with a specific stat spread, and that spread determines its evolution path later.

Your pet also has a house. Decorate it. Buy rooms. Give it shoes. Yes, shoes.

The economy: pets with bigger vaults produce more POOP. Gardens let you plant POOP to grow flowers. The power plant burns waste. Everything cycles.

But in-game you just see skill bars, food shelves, and a pet that needs feeding. No charts. No yield percentages.

Endgame: evolution branches, grid battles, multiple pets with different builds. Your burger-fed brawler fights differently than your salad-fed healer.

It's a Tamagotchi that quietly became an RPG.

Sprites are live. Eggs are cracking.

Feed your pet. Grow your garden. Plant real trees.
POOP is an in-game resource, not a financial product. NFT pets are digital collectibles for gameplay. Not financial advice. Tree planting verified on-chain via MfT ecosystem.
notes: Post as reply to any thread. Required per ethics review.
